blurb: Apple silicon integrates dedicated security hardware – including an AES cryptographic engine, Public Key Accelerator (PKA), True Random Number Generator (TRNG), and Secure Enclave – directly on the system-on-chip. Hardware security features such as memory encryption, secure Boot ROM, and device-unique UID keys are provisioned at manufacturing time and are permanently inaccessible to software. This silicon-rooted trust model ensures cryptographic operations and key material are physically isolated from the application processor, establishing the immutable foundation upon which all higher-level platform security protections are built.
